title: ferray-linalg: solve only accepts Ix2 for A and IxDyn for b — inconsistent typing
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

solve() requires a: &Array<T, Ix2> and b: &Array<T, IxDyn>. This means the caller must have A as a fixed-rank Ix2 array but b as dynamic. NumPy accepts any array-like for both. All other linalg functions also require specific dimension types (Ix2, Ix1), not IxDyn. This creates friction — callers with IxDyn arrays must first verify shape and convert. Consider accepting IxDyn for all inputs and validating shapes at runtime, or provide dual APIs (Ix2 for typed, IxDyn for dynamic).
